name: scenario_8ec00b_public_transportation_school_district_s_new_mesa_ind_col
premise: the incident command structure is under pressure in Q2 2026: 5 critical issues have surfaced over 48 hours, and the response will set a policy precedent for Public Transportation. The immediate incident is an operational integrity incident. The trigger is that competing obligations are colliding under time pressure, and the current playbook is not specific enough to be defensible. The board has pre-approved up to $3.0M in emergency spend if controls are documented, reversible, and auditable. Stakeholders frame this as individual vs collective: what looks efficient in aggregate may be unacceptable when it concentrates loss on a smaller group. Dashboards conflict with frontline notes, and vendor telemetry is delayed enough that decisions must be made on partial evidence. A decision is required before a 72-hour records production deadline. Boundaries: Any policy change must be reversible within 30 minutes. All exceptions require a written ticket with a reason code. Public communications must distinguish verified facts from hypotheses. Whatever choice is made will be cited in later incidents as the default playbook for this domain.

agents.name: Crisis Controller Sana Navarro
agents.identity: I am Crisis Controller Sana Navarro, coordinating the Public Transportation response at Mesa Public Transportation Coordination Cell. My mandate is to act fast without blurring accountability, to keep an auditable trail, and to make sure the costs of errors don’t land on people least able to contest them.
agents.goal: I want an operational memo that withstands regulator questioning: assumptions stated, risks assigned, rollback triggers defined.

agents.name: Patient Safety Liaison Mei Patel
agents.identity: I am Patient Safety Liaison Mei Patel, coordinating the Public Transportation response at Mesa Public Transportation Coordination Cell. My mandate is to act fast without blurring accountability, to keep an auditable trail, and to make sure the costs of errors don’t land on people least able to contest them.
agents.goal: I want transparency that separates verified facts from guesses and forces equitable review of who is harmed by defaults.

choices: I will authorize a narrow, time-boxed pilot first, with a reversible kill-switch, an appeals channel usable without counsel, and two-person sign-off for overrides; I will not scale until predefined safety and equity checks pass.
I will authorize a broad rollout immediately to reduce near-term harm, with transparent metrics and a rapid correction loop, accepting that some harms may surface only after precedent is set.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The window for a controlled decision is closing. Crisis Controller Sana Navarro must choose an authorization approach for this Public Transportation decision. What does Crisis Controller Sana Navarro do?
